items.hook: The Architect's Dream is an 1840 oil painting created by Thomas Cole for New York architect Ithiel Town.
items.body: Cole incorporated pieces of architecture from Egyptian, Greek, Roman, and Gothic styles in various different parts of the painting, having dabbled in architecture previously. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Soviet space dogs: Dezik and Tsygan were launched into a sub-orbital spaceflight from Kapustin Yar and became the first dogs to fly in space and the first to safely return.
items.body: In the 1950s and 1960s, the Soviet space program used dogs for suborbital and orbital space flights as proof-of-concept to determine whether human spaceflight was feasible. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Jim Ned High School is a public high school located in Tuscola, Texas, United States and is classified as a 3A school by the UIL.
items.body: It is part of the Jim Ned Consolidated Independent School District, which also includes Lawn Elementary, Buffalo Gap Elementary and Jim Ned Middle School. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Pass the pit diapers! Why are fans wetting themselves to stand front row at concerts?
items.body:
Olivia Rodrigo, Noah Kahan and Morgan Wallen have all dealt with audience effluence in recent weeks – but that’s only one part of a fearsome subculture that has built up around fandom, endurance and ‘getting barrier’

items.hook: Norway attacks: A bomb explodes, targeted at government buildings in central Oslo, followed by a massacre at a youth camp on the island of Utøya.
items.body: On 22 July 2011, 32-year-old Norwegian far-right terrorist Anders Behring Breivik committed two domestic terrorist attacks in Norway against the government, the civilian population, and a Workers' Youth League (AUF) summer camp, in which a total of 77 people were killed. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Avatar Aang: The Last Airbender review – engaging TV-to-movie adventure
items.body:
A feature-length continuation of the much-loved animated series provides enough for fans as well as the uninitiated

Avatar just can’t catch a break at the movies. Not the blue-cat-people franchise that makes at least a billion doll

items.hook: Preparedness Day Bombing: In San Francisco, a bomb explodes on Market Street during a parade, killing ten and injuring 40.
items.body: The Preparedness Day bombing was a bombing in San Francisco, California, United States, on July 22, 1916, of a parade organised by local supporters of the Preparedness Movement which advocated American entry into World War I. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Albany, New York is formally chartered as a municipality by Governor Thomas Dongan.
items.body: Albany is the capital city of the U.S. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Natsuko Imamura is a Japanese writer.
items.body: She has been nominated three times for the Akutagawa Prize, and won the prize in 2019. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The 1944 Lehigh Engineers football team was an American football team that represented Lehigh University during the 1944 college football season.
items.body: In its second season under head coach Leo Prendergast, the team compiled an 0–6 record, including four losses against Middle Three Conference rivals. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Ottoman wars in Europe: Siege of Belgrade: John Hunyadi, Regent of the Kingdom of Hungary, defeats Mehmet II of the Ottoman Empire.
items.body: A series of military conflicts between the Ottoman Empire and various European states took place from the Late Middle Ages up through the early 20th century. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: First Crusade: Godfrey of Bouillon is elected the first Defender of the Holy Sepulchre of The Kingdom of Jerusalem.
items.body: The First Crusade (1096–1099) was the first of a series of religious wars, or Crusades, which were initiated, supported and at times directed by the Latin Church in the Middle Ages. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Waipā District Council is the territorial authority for the Waipā District of New Zealand.
items.body: It serves as the district's local government alongside the Waikato Regional Council as the regional council. One of those things you can't un-know.
